name: GLM-5.2 (Max)
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts, 744B total / 40B active, DSA + 'IndexShare' sparse-attention indexer reuse (2.9x fewer per-token FLOPs at 1M context), 1M-token context; selectable High/Max reasoning-effort modes
note: Highest-capability reasoning-effort tier: raises the thinking-token budget (~85k tokens on hard tasks vs High's ~50% of that) for complex, long-horizon agentic problems. Same weights as the High tier.

results.notes: Humanity's Last Exam, no-tools; with-tools variant reaches 54.7
SWE-bench Pro; matches figure independently repeated on the HF blog
Terminal-Bench 2.1 (Terminus-2 harness), vs Claude Opus 4.8's 85.0 in the same table; a 'best reported' config reaches 82.7 per the HF blog
Artificial Analysis's independent IFBench measurement for GLM-5.2 (max), surfaced via the BenchLM.ai AA-data mirror. Not reported by Zhipu/Z.ai itself.
Artificial Analysis's own GDPval-AA v2 re-evaluation of GLM-5.2 (max) (Elo-like 0-2000 scale), from AA's dedicated launch-analysis article. Independent of Zhipu's self-reported numbers.

verificationNotes: See GLM-5.2 (High) entry for shared model-level verification (existence, dates, params, license). This entry carries the publicly reported self-reported benchmark scores, on the assumption (not fully confirmed by Z.ai) that the published numbers reflect the top/Max reasoning-effort configuration -- Z.ai did not publish an explicit per-tier breakdown at launch per MarkTechPost's coverage. Scores cross-checked between huggingface.co/blog/zai-org/glm-52-blog (HLE, AIME, GPQA, and an extended coding-benchmark table) and official docs.z.ai/guides/llm/glm-5.2 (Terminal-Bench 2.1 81.0 vs Claude Opus 4.8's 85.0, SWE-bench Pro 62.1, FrontierSWE within 1% of Opus 4.8) -- both agree on SWE-bench Pro 62.1 and Terminal-Bench 2.1 ~81.0, giving good confidence in those two figures. Omitted CritPt (16.7), HMMT Nov 2025 (94.4), HMMT Feb 2026 (92.5), IMOAnswerBench (91.0), NL2Repo (48.9), DeepSWE (46.2), ProgramBench (63.7), FrontierSWE (74.4), PostTrainBench (34.3), SWE-Marathon (13.0), MCP-Atlas (76.8), and Tool-Decathlon (48.2) for lack of a matching corpus benchmark slug.
